title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego conceded all four Guardians' runs in the first inning, resulting in a Red Sox 4-3 loss.
Brayan Bello excelled in bulk relief, pitching seven scoreless innings without walks, striking out five.
Red Sox now sit at 23-33, ten games under .500, after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are the top teams linked to landing Antetokounmpo, who wants to stay in the East.
Speculation involves whether Jaylen Brown could be included in a trade for Antetokounmpo to the Celtics.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns and students attempting to swim.
The gathering involved hundreds from the Harvard Class of 2026 and took place a day after their commencement ceremony.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Massachusetts State police responded to clear the bridge; no reports of arrests or injuries yet.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is streaming on Peacock, offering a single-movie format of Tarantino's original vision.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, featuring Tina Fey and Steve Carell, is available on Netflix.
'Spider-Noir' starring Nicolas Cage is now streaming on Prime Video in both color and black-and-white formats.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman Bo Lowrance in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, compared to Freddie Freeman by scouts, is a left-handed hitter committed to the University of Virginia.
Bo Lowrance, from South Carolina, stands 6-foot-5 and is lauded for his smooth stroke and consistent contact ability.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, of Lowell, charged with concealing over $48 million in cash payroll taxes.
The alleged scheme involved cash payments to workers via Worcester check-cashing business from 2019 to 2025.
Charges include conspiracy and tax offenses, each carrying potential five-year prison sentences and significant fines.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is seeking a contract extension to remain with the New England Patriots during OTAs.
Gonzalez's current Patriots contract pays $4 million; he wants $32-35 million annually, according to Tom Curran.
The Patriots restructured Mike Onwenu's contract, clearing cap space potentially for Gonzalez's extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect in a drug investigation led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham before escaping.
The suspect, wanted on multiple arrest warrants, struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer.
The vehicle was found abandoned in Stoneham, and the driver and passenger fled on foot, prompting an ongoing search.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, became managing director of Fifth Down Capital in February.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and retail sectors.
Mayo was fired after a 4-13 Patriots season in 2024, following a stint at Optum.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews of Massachusetts set a new speed record for climbing Mount Everest with supplemental oxygen in 9 hours, 55 minutes.
Andrews surpassed Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa's 2003 record of 10 hours, 56 minutes, pending official verification.
This achievement marks Andrews' seventh attempt at the record and honors his late friend, Greg Krupa.
